english: pf. zad (zad) 1. to be on the decline, pf. to be consumed, spent, frq., bságs-pai nor °dzad (bsags pa'i nor 'dzad) the gathered wealth goes to an end Pth.; snúm-zad-kyi már-me (snum zad kyi mar me) a lamp the oil of which is exhausted Glr.; k'yód-kyi bsód-nams zád-pai tsóṅ-p'rug-rnams ye (khyod kyi bsod nams zad pa'i tshong phrug rnams ye) (poor) partners in trade, whose stored-up merits are now at an end (whilst the speaker by the strength of his virtue is saved from the danger in which the others perish) Glr.; rgyágs-la zad (rgyags la zad) that has been spent for provisions Mil.; brlai śa zad kyaṅ yaṅ-ṅo (brla'i sha zad kyang yang ngo) the flesh of the upper part of the thigh, even after it had been used (after all had been laid on the scales), was nevertheless lighter than . . . Dzl.; ťabs-zád (thabs zad) helpless Glr.; tse-yóṅs-su zád-pa-las (tshe yongs su zad pa las) whilst life is consuming itself Do.; tse-zád-kar (tshe zad kar) Do., prob. the same as °ćí-k'ar ('chi khar), at the hour of death; frq. referred to sin: °dód-čágs-kyi sems, dri-ma kun, nyés-pai skyon ťams-čád, °dód-pa kun yóṅs-su zád-de ('dod chags kyi sems, dri ma kun, nyes pa'i skyon thams cad, 'dod pa kun yongs su zad de) sensuality and all sin, desire and defilement being done away with, having ceased Dzl.; dug lṅai lás-la zád-pa med (dug lnga'i las la zad pa med) the effects of the five poisons (q.v.) never cease; °dré-la zad-pa med ('dre la zad pa med) of devils there is an infinite number Mil.; zad(-pa) méd(-pa) (zad (-pa) med (-pa)), zad-mi-śes-pa (zad mi shes pa) incessant, endless, everlasting — 2. °dis zad ('dis zad) with this it is done, i.e. a. this is the only thing, besides which no second is existing; °dis don-gnyer-źiṅ °tsó-bar zád-na ('dis don gnyer zhing 'tsho bar zad na) as this is our only means of making a living Dzl.; bu ni k'yod gčig-pur zád-de (bu ni khyod gcig pur zad de) as thou art our only son Dzl.; mťóṅ-ba k'ó-mo k'ó-nar zád-de (mthong ba kho mo kho nar zad de) as I am the only person that has seen . . . Tar.; mťsóṅ-bar zád-de (mtshong bar zad de) this is limited to seeing, this refers only to sight Dzl. LV, 12; gnyis ni miṅ gčig-pa tsám-du zád-pas (gnyis ni ming gcig pa tsam du zad pas) as the two have only one name Tar.; hence the frequent ma zád-de (ma zad de) with the termin. case, not only, srog °dór-ba °di °bá-źig-tu ma zád-de (srog 'dor ba 'di 'ba' zhig tu ma zad de) having lost his life not only this time (but often so before) Dzl. VZ, 13; der ma zad(-kyi) (der ma zad (-kyi)) not enough with that, still more, further, yea even Thgy. — b. it is decided, settled, unquestionable, nor rgyál-pos bźés-par zád-na (nor rgyal pos bzhes par zad na) as the fortune unquestionably falls to the king.
magic sentence, bzlá-ba (bzla ba) to pronounce one Lex.
to strive, endeavour; to be studious, to give diligence Sch.
gun, cannon, *gyáb-pa* (*gyab pa*) C. to discharge.

Cs.: 'to hang down'; yet it is evidently the prop. present-form to the pf. bzar (bzar) and the fut. gzar (gzar), which frq. are used without regard to tense: to hang up, clothes on a line Dzl.; to hang or throw over, the toga over one's shoulder Dzl. and elsewh.
to abstain from, to be abstinent, temperate Sch.
to quarrel, contend, fight, mče-, sder-, rwa-°dziṅ byéd-pa (mche-, sder-, rwa-'dzing byed pa) to fight with tusks, claws, horns Cs.; °dziṅ-mo ('dzing mo) quarrel, contention, dispute.
gen. with skra (skra), rarely with mgo (mgo) Glr., bristly, rugged, shaggy, of beggars Dzl., infernal monsters Dzl. — sprín-sna °dziṅs-mťiṅ-nág (sprin sna 'dzings mthing nag) Mil.?

1. the act of seizing, seizure, grasp, gripe, v. °dzin-pa ('dzin pa), e.g. nyi-°dzin (nyi 'dzin) eclipse of the sun, zla-°dzin (zla 'dzin) lunar eclipse, (the heavenly bodies being seized by the dragon Rāhula, v. sgra-gčan (sgra gcan)), ril-°dzin (ril 'dzin) total, ča-°dzin (cha 'dzin) partial eclipse Wdk. — 2. he that seizes, holds fast, a holder, keeper; receptacle; rdo-rje-°dzin (rdo rje 'dzin) v. rdó-rje (rdo rje); ču-°dzin (chu 'dzin) po. cloud, ro-°dzin (ro 'dzin) po. tongue Lex.; adherent, e.g. in srol-°dzin (srol 'dzin). — 3. bond, obligation, certificate, e.g. p'rod-°dzin (phrod 'dzin) receipt, acquittance. — 4. contract, agreement, treaty, *źág-pa* (*zhag pa*) C., *táṅ-če* (*thang ce*) W., to conclude, make, a bargain, a treaty; yig-°dzin (yig 'dzin) a written agreement.

I. vb. pf. (b)zuṅ ((b)zung), fut. gzuṅ (gzung), imp. zuṅ(s) (zung(s)), also gzúṅ-ba (gzung ba), bzúṅ-ba (bzung ba) and zín-pa (zin pa) in all tenses, W. *zúm-če* (*zum ce*), Bal. *zún-čas* (*zun chas*), 1. to take hold of, to seize, grasp,
